titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas Maguire to Henry Arthur Wynne, 19 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Henry Arthur Wynne

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Thomas Maguire (b.1863), solicitor to Henry Arthur Wynne (1867-1943), the chief crown solicitor for Ireland. The letter regards the death of Helena Doyle (1866-1916). He states that Doyle was a spinster and that, as far as he knows her estate comes to £18. 10. 0, however her funeral and hospital costs have come to £19. Maguire wishes to inform Wynne about his inability to find any next of kin, requesting instruction regarding the issue.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5611/8622/2

correspAction.placeName: Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The Chief Crown Solicitor
Chief Secretary's office
DUBLIN CASTLE 19th May 1916.
Re Miss Helena Doyle Deceased. Dear Sir, The above, who I understand was a Spinster and lived at
Brookfield Street Belfast, died at the Mater Hospital, Belfast, on 11th
January 1916, Intestate So far as I can see from the papers handed to me the whole
estate, consisting of Policies of Assurance and small balance in Savings
Bank, amounts to about £18. 10. 0, and the claims against the estate,
Funeral expenses, Hospital charges, and incidental expenses after death,
come to about £19. 0. 0. Up to the present I have not been able to trace and next of kin. I shall be glad to have your instruction touching the
administration of the estate. Yours faithfully, Tho.Maguire
